name: Gmail Monitor
description: Monitor Gmail inbox — unread count and new email detection with sender, subject, and language tagging
readme:
# Gmail Monitor

Monitors your Gmail inbox in real time and surfaces unread counts and new email metadata to your Orchestra AI agents.

## What it tracks

- **Unread email count** — badge value from the Gmail sidebar, updated on every inbox change
- **New emails** — up to 5 most recent unread threads with sender address, subject line, preview snippet (100 chars), and detected language

## How it works

The plugin injects a passive content script into `mail.google.com`. It attaches `MutationObserver` watchers to the unread badge and conversation list. Changes are debounced (1.5–2 s) and sent to the Orchestra service worker only when the value has actually changed — no duplicate events.

## Events emitted

| Event | Payload |
|---|---|
| `GMAIL_UNREAD_COUNT` | `{ count, url }` |
| `GMAIL_NEW_EMAILS` | `{ emails: [{ sender, subject, preview, lang }], count }` |

## Privacy

Only metadata is captured — sender address, subject, and a 100-character preview. Email body content is never read or transmitted.

name: Slack Monitor
description: Monitor Slack workspace — unread channel count, mention count, and new message detection with sender and language tagging
readme:
# Slack Monitor

Monitors your Slack workspace for unread activity and new messages, keeping your Orchestra agents informed of team communication.

## What it tracks

- **Unread channel count** — number of channels with unread messages in the sidebar
- **Mention count** — total `@mention` badges summed across all channels
- **New messages** — most recent message with sender name, text (200 chars), language, and current channel path

## How it works

The plugin attaches two `MutationObserver` instances: one on the sidebar (for unread/mention counts) and one on the virtual message list (for new message detection). Changes are debounced and de-duplicated before being forwarded to Orchestra.

## Events emitted

| Event | Payload |
|---|---|
| `SLACK_UNREAD_SUMMARY` | `{ unreadChannelCount, mentionCount }` |
| `SLACK_NEW_MESSAGE` | `{ sender, text, lang, channel }` |

## Privacy

Only the last visible message is captured — sender name and a 200-character text snippet. Message history is never bulk-read.

name: Discord Monitor
description: Monitor Discord — unread channel count and mention badge totals across all servers and DMs
readme:
# Discord Monitor

Monitors your Discord client for unread channels and mention badges across all servers and direct messages.

## What it tracks

- **Unread channel count** — total elements with unread CSS class across all visible servers
- **Mention count** — sum of all numeric badge values (server pings, DM pings, @mentions)

## How it works

Discord heavily obfuscates its CSS class names with hashes. The plugin uses class-prefix matching (`[class*="unread"]`, `[class*="badge"]`) to survive Discord's frequent UI rebuilds. A MutationObserver on the sidebar fires on any DOM or attribute change, debounced to 2 seconds.

## Events emitted

| Event | Payload |
|---|---|
| `DISCORD_UNREAD_SUMMARY` | `{ unreadChannels, mentionCount, url }` |

## Privacy

Only counts are captured — no message content, usernames, or server names are read or transmitted.

name: WhatsApp Monitor
description: Monitor WhatsApp Web — total unread message count and per-chat breakdown of active conversations
readme:
# WhatsApp Monitor

Monitors WhatsApp Web for unread messages and surfaces a per-chat breakdown to your Orchestra agents.

## What it tracks

- **Total unread count** — sum of all unread badge values across the chat list
- **Per-chat breakdown** — up to 5 chats with their title and individual unread count
- **Chat count** — number of conversations with unread messages

## How it works

The plugin observes the WhatsApp Web chat list panel using a `MutationObserver`. When the unread badge total changes, it collects individual chat titles and counts and sends a summary event. Debounced to 2 seconds to avoid flooding during rapid notification arrivals.

## Events emitted

| Event | Payload |
|---|---|
| `WHATSAPP_UNREAD_SUMMARY` | `{ totalUnread, chatCount, chats: [{ title, count }] }` |

## Privacy

Only chat names and unread counts are captured. Message content is never read or transmitted.

name: Telegram Monitor
description: Monitor Telegram Web — total unread message count and active chat count across all conversations
readme:
# Telegram Monitor

Monitors Telegram Web for unread messages across all chats, groups, and channels.

## What it tracks

- **Total unread count** — sum of all numeric badge values in the chat list
- **Chat count** — number of conversations with active unread badges

## How it works

A `MutationObserver` on the `.chatlist-container` element fires whenever chats are added, removed, or updated. Badge values are parsed and summed. An event is only emitted when the total changes.

## Events emitted

| Event | Payload |
|---|---|
| `TELEGRAM_UNREAD_SUMMARY` | `{ totalUnread, chatCount, url }` |

## Privacy

Only unread counts and chat counts are captured. Chat names and message content are never read or transmitted.

name: X / Twitter Monitor
description: Monitor X (Twitter) — notification count and direct message count from the navigation tab bar
readme:
# X / Twitter Monitor

Monitors X (formerly Twitter) for unread notification and direct message counts.

## What it tracks

- **Notification count** — badge value on the Notifications tab bar link
- **DM count** — badge value on the Messages tab bar link

## How it works

The plugin observes the navigation tab bar for DOM and attribute mutations. It also patches `history.pushState` to re-check counts after SPA navigation, ensuring accuracy when the user switches between pages.

## Events emitted

| Event | Payload |
|---|---|
| `TWITTER_NOTIFICATION_SUMMARY` | `{ notificationCount, dmCount, url }` |

## Privacy

Only badge counts are captured from the navigation bar. Tweet content, follower data, and timeline content are never read.

name: GitHub Monitor
description: Monitor GitHub — notification count, unread status, and PR/issue context when viewing pull requests and issues
readme:
# GitHub Monitor

Monitors GitHub for unread notifications and extracts contextual PR/issue data when you open a specific pull request or issue page.

## What it tracks

- **Notification count** — unread notification badge in the GitHub header
- **Notification list** — total and unread count when on the `/notifications` page
- **PR/Issue context** — owner, repo, type, number, title, state, and review count when viewing a PR or issue

## How it works

The plugin watches the `<title>` element for changes (GitHub updates the title on navigation). It also patches `history.pushState` and `popstate` to handle SPA navigation. PR/issue data is extracted from URL path matching and DOM selectors.

## Events emitted

| Event | Payload |
|---|---|
| `GITHUB_NOTIFICATIONS` | `{ count, hasUnread, url }` |
| `GITHUB_NOTIFICATION_LIST` | `{ total, unread }` |
| `GITHUB_PR_CONTEXT` | `{ owner, repo, type, number, title, state, reviews, url }` |

## Privacy

Only notification counts and PR/issue metadata visible on the current page are captured. Repository code and file contents are never read.

name: Linear Monitor
description: Monitor Linear — inbox notification count and current issue context when viewing individual issues
readme:
# Linear Monitor

Monitors Linear for inbox notifications and surfaces issue context when you navigate to individual issues.

## What it tracks

- **Inbox count** — number of unread inbox items (uses the badge count or DOM item count as fallback)
- **Issue context** — issue ID (e.g. `ENG-123`) and title when viewing a specific issue page

## How it works

The plugin observes the body element for top-level DOM changes and patches `history.pushState` for SPA navigation. Issue ID is extracted from the URL path via regex, and the page title is cleaned of the " - Linear" suffix.

## Events emitted

| Event | Payload |
|---|---|
| `LINEAR_INBOX_COUNT` | `{ count, url }` |
| `LINEAR_ISSUE_VIEWED` | `{ issueId, title, url }` |

## Privacy

Only the inbox count and issue ID/title visible in the browser tab are captured. Issue descriptions, comments, and team data are never read.

name: Jira Monitor
description: Monitor Jira — issue context when viewing individual issues and card count on sprint boards
readme:
# Jira Monitor

Monitors Jira for issue context and sprint board activity as you navigate through your projects.

## What it tracks

- **Issue context** — issue key (e.g. `PROJ-42`), title, and status when viewing an issue at `/browse/PROJ-42`
- **Board card count** — total number of cards visible on a sprint board when navigating to a `/boards` page

## How it works

The plugin patches `history.pushState` and `popstate` to handle Jira's SPA navigation. Issue key is extracted from the URL path; title and status are read from Atlassian's `data-testid` attributes which are stable across Jira updates.

## Events emitted

| Event | Payload |
|---|---|
| `JIRA_ISSUE_VIEWED` | `{ issueKey, title, status, url }` |
| `JIRA_BOARD_VIEWED` | `{ cardCount, url }` |

## Privacy

Only issue key, title, and status are captured — all visible in the page title and status badge. Issue descriptions, comments, and attachments are never read.

name: Google Calendar Monitor
description: Monitor Google Calendar — event count loaded in the current view, helping agents stay aware of your schedule
readme:
# Google Calendar Monitor

Monitors Google Calendar and tells your Orchestra agents how many events are loaded in the current calendar view.

## What it tracks

- **Event count** — total number of visible events in the current calendar view (day/week/month)
- **Today event count** — up to 10 events from the current view snapshot

## How it works

Google Calendar re-renders the event grid on every navigation. The plugin attaches a `MutationObserver` to `document.body` for top-level DOM changes and re-counts `[data-eventid]` elements each time. The event is only emitted when the count changes.

## Events emitted

| Event | Payload |
|---|---|
| `GCAL_EVENTS_LOADED` | `{ eventCount, todayEventCount, url }` |

## Privacy

Only the event count and event IDs are captured. Event titles, attendees, descriptions, and meeting links are never read.

name: Cal.com Monitor
description: Monitor Cal.com — total booking count and upcoming booking count on your bookings dashboard
readme:
# Cal.com Monitor

Monitors your Cal.com bookings dashboard for total and upcoming booking counts.

## What it tracks

- **Total bookings** — all booking items visible on the current page
- **Upcoming count** — bookings inside the `[data-testid='upcoming']` section

## How it works

The plugin observes the `<main>` element for DOM changes (new bookings loaded, pagination). When the total booking count changes, a summary event is sent. Debounced to 2 seconds.

## Events emitted

| Event | Payload |
|---|---|
| `CALCOM_BOOKINGS_LOADED` | `{ totalBookings, upcomingCount, url }` |

## Privacy

Only booking counts are captured. Attendee names, email addresses, and booking details are never read.

name: Google Meet Monitor
description: Monitor Google Meet — meeting detection, participant tracking, and opt-in live caption extraction with speaker attribution
readme:
# Google Meet Monitor

Monitors Google Meet for meeting lifecycle events, participant changes, and — with explicit user opt-in — live caption transcription with speaker attribution.

## What it tracks

- **Meeting detection** — fires when participant tiles or the call toolbar appear
- **Meeting start/end** — sends timestamped events with title, participants, and a local meeting ID
- **Participant updates** — emits when the participant count changes during an active meeting
- **Live captions (opt-in)** — after user grants permission, extracts speaker name and caption text in real time with language detection

## How it works

The plugin uses a `MutationObserver` on `document.body` to detect when the meeting UI mounts or unmounts. Participant names are collected from tile `[data-participant-id]` elements and the people panel sidebar. Caption extraction uses multiple selector fallbacks to survive Google Meet UI updates.

## Events emitted

| Event | Payload |
|---|---|
| `meeting_detected` | `{ platform, title, participants, meetingId, url }` |
| `meeting_started` | `{ platform, title, participants, meetingId, url, ts }` |
| `meeting_ended` | `{ platform, meetingId, url, ts }` |
| `GMEET_PARTICIPANT_UPDATE` | `{ participantCount, participants, url }` |
| `caption` (opt-in) | `{ meetingId, speaker, text, lang, ts }` |

## Privacy

Caption transcription is strictly opt-in. The service worker displays a consent prompt before any caption data is forwarded. Meeting detection and participant counts are sent automatically, but no audio or video data is ever accessed.

name: Zoom Monitor
description: Monitor Zoom web client — meeting detection, participant tracking, recording status, upcoming meetings, and opt-in live caption transcription
readme:
# Zoom Monitor

Monitors the Zoom web client (`app.zoom.us`) for meeting lifecycle events, participant changes, recording status, upcoming meetings, and — with explicit user opt-in — live caption transcription.

## What it tracks

- **Meeting detection** — fires when video tiles or meeting controls appear in the DOM
- **Meeting start/end** — timestamped events with title, participants, recording status, and local meeting ID
- **Participant updates** — emits when participant count changes during an active call
- **Recording status** — included in meeting_started and participant update events
- **Upcoming meetings** — count of scheduled meetings on the Zoom home page
- **Live captions (opt-in)** — after user grants permission, extracts speaker and caption text with language detection

## How it works

The plugin patches `history.pushState` and `popstate` for SPA navigation and attaches a body-level `MutationObserver` for meeting UI mounting. Caption extraction supports Zoom Web SDK, the classic caption overlay, and the live transcription panel via multiple selector fallbacks.

## Events emitted

| Event | Payload |
|---|---|
| `meeting_detected` | `{ platform, title, participants, meetingId, url }` |
| `meeting_started` | `{ platform, title, participants, meetingId, isRecording, url, ts }` |
| `meeting_ended` | `{ platform, meetingId, url, ts }` |
| `ZOOM_PARTICIPANT_UPDATE` | `{ participantCount, participants, isRecording, url }` |
| `ZOOM_UPCOMING_MEETINGS` | `{ count, url }` |
| `caption` (opt-in) | `{ meetingId, speaker, text, lang, ts }` |

## Privacy

Caption transcription is strictly opt-in. The service worker displays a consent prompt before any caption data is forwarded. No audio or video streams are accessed.

name: GCP Billing Monitor
description: Monitor Google Cloud Platform billing — total cost, per-service breakdown, and project name from the GCP billing console
readme:
# GCP Billing Monitor

Monitors the Google Cloud Platform billing console and forwards your current cloud spend to Orchestra agents for cost awareness and alerting.

## What it tracks

- **Total cost** — current billing period total from the billing overview summary
- **Currency** — the billing currency code (default: USD)
- **Per-service breakdown** — up to 10 services from the cost table with service name and cost
- **Project name** — the GCP billing account or project name

## How it works

The plugin only activates on GCP pages that include `/billing` in the path. It observes the billing overview container for DOM changes and re-extracts cost data. SPA navigation is handled by patching `history.pushState`. Cost values are parsed from text using a regex that strips currency symbols and commas.

## Events emitted

| Event | Payload |
|---|---|
| `cost_update` | `{ source: 'gcp', type: 'cost_update', data: { total, currency, breakdown, project, url } }` |

## Privacy

Only cost figures and service names visible in the billing table are captured. Resource IDs, API keys, and project configurations are never read.

name: Claude Usage Monitor
description: Monitor Claude.ai usage — subscription plan, tokens used, credits remaining, billing period, and total cost from the settings page
readme:
# Claude Usage Monitor

Monitors your Claude.ai settings/usage page to keep Orchestra agents aware of your AI token consumption and billing status.

## What it tracks

- **Plan** — subscription tier (Free, Pro, Team, etc.)
- **Tokens used** — total tokens consumed in the current billing period
- **Credits remaining** — remaining credit balance
- **Billing period** — current period or reset date label
- **Total cost** — amount due or total billed

## How it works

The plugin only activates on `/settings` and `/usage` paths on `claude.ai`. It observes the usage container for DOM changes and uses multiple selector fallbacks (data-testid attributes and class-name partial matches) to survive UI updates. Values are parsed from text content using a number extraction regex.

## Events emitted

| Event | Payload |
|---|---|
| `cost_update` | `{ source: 'claude', type: 'cost_update', data: { plan, tokens_used, credits_remaining, period, total, url } }` |

## Privacy

Only usage numbers and plan name are captured from the settings page. Conversation content, API keys, and account details are never read.

name: OpenAI Usage Monitor
description: Monitor OpenAI Platform usage — total cost, daily average, API call count, credit balance, and billing period from the usage dashboard
readme:
# OpenAI Usage Monitor

Monitors the OpenAI Platform usage dashboard to keep Orchestra agents aware of your API spend and credit balance.

## What it tracks

- **Total cost** — current period total from the usage summary
- **Daily average** — computed from the daily usage table rows
- **API call count** — total API requests in the period
- **Credit balance** — remaining prepaid credit balance
- **Billing period** — current period label

## How it works

The plugin only activates on `/usage` and `/billing` paths on `platform.openai.com`. It observes the usage container for DOM changes and computes the daily average by parsing all visible daily cost table rows. Multiple selector fallbacks handle OpenAI's periodic UI updates.

## Events emitted

| Event | Payload |
|---|---|
| `cost_update` | `{ source: 'openai', type: 'cost_update', data: { total, daily_avg, api_calls, credit_balance, period, url } }` |

## Privacy

Only aggregated usage figures are captured from the dashboard. API keys, organization details, and model outputs are never read.

name: Perplexity Usage Monitor
description: Monitor Perplexity.ai usage — subscription plan, Pro queries used and remaining, and renewal date from the account settings page
readme:
# Perplexity Usage Monitor

Monitors your Perplexity.ai account settings page to track Pro query consumption and subscription renewal date.

## What it tracks

- **Plan** — subscription plan name (Free, Pro, etc.)
- **Queries used** — Pro queries consumed in the current period (falls back to progress bar `aria-valuenow`)
- **Queries remaining** — remaining Pro queries (computed from bar max - used if DOM element not found)
- **Renewal date** — next billing or reset date

## How it works

The plugin only activates on `/settings` and `/account` paths on `perplexity.ai`. It reads both explicit DOM elements and falls back to ARIA attributes on progress bars for resilience. Changes are de-duplicated and debounced to 2 seconds.

## Events emitted

| Event | Payload |
|---|---|
| `cost_update` | `{ source: 'perplexity', type: 'cost_update', data: { plan, queries_used, queries_remaining, renewal_date, url } }` |

## Privacy

Only plan name, query counts, and renewal date are captured. Search history and conversation content are never read.

name: X Premium Monitor
description: Monitor X Premium subscription — plan name, subscription status, renewal date, and monthly cost from the X settings page
readme:
# X Premium Monitor

Monitors your X (Twitter) Premium subscription settings to keep Orchestra aware of your subscription status and billing cycle.

## What it tracks

- **Plan name** — subscription tier (Basic, Premium, Premium+)
- **Status** — subscription status (Active, Cancelled, Past Due, etc.)
- **Renewal date** — next billing date
- **Monthly cost** — subscription price parsed from the billing amount

## How it works

The plugin only activates on `/premium` and `/settings/subscription` paths on `x.com`. It observes the subscription container for DOM changes and patches `history.pushState` to re-check path after SPA navigation. An event is only emitted when the payload actually changes.

## Events emitted

| Event | Payload |
|---|---|
| `cost_update` | `{ source: 'x-premium', type: 'cost_update', data: { plan, status, renewal_date, monthly_cost, url } }` |

## Privacy

Only subscription metadata visible on the settings page is captured. Timeline content, DMs, and account credentials are never read.
